q: The normal force exerted by a banked road is _______ to the road.
An attractive force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction _______ to the path of the earth.
An object moving in a circle must have a _______ applied to it to keep it moving in the circle.
The centrifugal force is an apparent or _______ force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
When we swing a ball on the end of a string in a circle, the _______ in the string provides the required centripetal force.
The upward thrust acts _______ to a flying aeroplane body.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the _______ force acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane.
Banking of roads is raising the _______ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
The concept of the centrifugal force can be applied in _______ devices.
When whirling a body attached to a string, a _______ force is originated.
If the _______ force is not sufficient, the car going in the unbanked curved road will skid out of the circular path.
In a laboratory centrifuge, the _______ acceleration causes the denser particles to settle to the bottom.
When a car moves in a curved circular path, the _______ component of the normal force is the required centripetal force.
In a banked curved road, the angle that the inclined track makes with the _______ is called the angle of banking.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the _______ component of the normal force acts as the required centripetal force.
When a car makes a turn on an unbanked road, the car will skid out of the circular path if the _______ force is not sufficient.
When an automobile drives round a curve, it must have a force exerted towards the _______ of the curve.
An object that is accelerating must have a _______ acting on it.
_______ force is the force acting on an object in circular motion which is directed towards the axis of rotation or centre of curvature.
The direction of the _______ acceleration is towards the centre of the circle.
The force that enables an object to keep it moving in the circle is the _______ force.
The upward thrust acting on a flying plane is also known as the _______ force.
Removing the excess water by spin drying clothes in a washing machine is done with the help of _______ force.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the frictional force acts _______ to the direction of the motion of the car.
In a banked curved road, the _______ component of the normal force provides the required centripetal force.
A centrifugal pump converts mechanical energy into _______ energy.
The _______ force appears to act in a direction pointing away from the centre.
The _______ action of a washing machine is used in spin drying the clothes.
The process that causes denser particles to settle to the bottom is called _______ principle.
The curved road must be banked to provide the necessary _______ force to the automobiles.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the reaction force of the road acts _______ to the car.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a _______ force exists between the road and the car tyres.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and regulate the _______ of a machine.
The centrifugal governor is a device that uses the advantage of the _______ force.
For uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration vector points towards the _______ of the circular path.
The banking angle of a road is chosen so that this condition holds for the _______ speed.
The _______ force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path around which the object is moving.
In a washing machine, the washed clothes are spun very fast and excess water is removed completely with the help of _______ force.
When a car makes a turn on an unbanked road, the _______ force acting upon the tyres of the car provides the required centripetal force for circular motion.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the _______ force.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the _______ force provides the required centripetal force.
For uniform circular motion, the acceleration is _______ acceleration.
On an unbanked road, the _______ force is supplied by friction between the tyres and the road.
The centripetal force is _______ to the direction of the motion of the body in circular motion.
When a car moves around a curve, the person in the car feels an _______ force exerted on him.
A _______ is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed axis.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the frictional force behaves as a _______ force.
The centrifugal force is caused by the effect of _______.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the _______ force provides the required centripetal force.
